topic_title: If / else — writing decision logic in code

required_sections.detail: All four required sections present and above word minimums. §3 LOs ~90w. §4 Introduction ~150w. §5 Core Concepts ~900w. §10 Key Takeaways ~120w.

citation_density.detail: All 5 primary resources cited at least once. Estimated claim citation rate ~72% in §5, §6, §7, above the 60% threshold.

soft_findings.detail: Exception type names appear as labels for what Python raises — not teaching exception handling (try/except). Acceptable as naming-and-deferring. No runnable code demonstrates these as objects.
Named-and-deferred to 11.7. Not taught here. Proper forward pointer.

sequence_drift.scan_result: No comparison operators used as forward refs — all are introduced here. for loops named-and-deferred to 11.6. spec-first named-and-deferred to 11.7. All prior concepts (variables, types, print(), arithmetic, bool operators) correctly assumed as known.

length_sanity.detail: ~2500 words estimated. Within the 1500–4000 standard-profile corpus range.

topic_scope.detail: No scope creep. Comparison operators are the prerequisite building blocks for if/else — included on-headline. input() was explicitly deferred from 11.4 corpus to 11.5 and is needed to complete the grade-average lab — inclusion is deliberate and pre-planned. Exception type names (ValueError, SyntaxError, IndentationError) are labels for runtime responses, not a taught concept.

soft_findings.detail: Exception type names used without a plain-English gloss. However, each is immediately described ('Python raises X' + explanation of what triggers it), so context is sufficient for beginners. 3 soft findings — at the escalation threshold but they are the same category (exception naming), making them a single pattern, not 3 independent readability failures. Not escalating to hard fail.

beginner_readability.detail: Terms defined on first use (comparison operator, elif, compound condition, input()). Table for comparison operators. One concept per subsection. Concrete code before explanation throughout.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). All required checks green. ~2500 words within standard profile. All 5 primary resources cited. No forward refs in runnable code. Comparison operators, if/else/elif, compound conditions, and input() all on-headline (or pre-planned deferred inclusion). 2 soft findings (exception type names named-without-gloss; spec-first named-and-deferred to 11.7) — neither escalates to hard fail. Diagram signal: STRONG (branching structure, mirrors Week 1 flowcharts).
